name: Fruit Crescents
recipeInstructions:
Raspberry preserves can be made easily using any variety of fruit preserves.
1. In a large bowl, mix together the softened cream cheese and butter with flour until a smooth dough forms.
2. Chill the dough for about 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Roll out the chilled dough on wax paper to a thickness of about 1/8 - 1/4 inch.
5. Cut the rolled dough into 3-inch squares.
6. Place approximately 1 teaspoon of preserves slightly off-center on each square.
7. Fold the dough over to enclose the preserves, forming a triangle shape without completely reaching the edge with the top flap.
8. Roll the dough toward the point and smooth and seal the edges.
9. Bend in the ends of the rolled dough to create a crescent shape.
10. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 25-30 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and cool on wire racks before rolling in confectioners sugar.
Yield: Approximately 2 dozen
